commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=84237e0a411e86d49f7fef5452cff48cfe438010)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a macOS Talk gateway-relay realtime path, a trusted `chat.send.runtimePromptContext` field, relay proof log markers, regression tests, and Talk docs updates.

PR surface: Source +59, Tests +77, Docs +5, Other +1045. Total +1186 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows PR-head macOS sends a field that current main's strict `chat.send` schema does not accept. The feature itself is not a bug report.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor or maintainer changes plus signed app proof; automation cannot supply the missing real macOS runtime evidence.

target.title: feat(talk): add macOS realtime relay mode
target.body:
## Summary
- add a provider-neutral macOS Talk realtime relay path backed by `talk.session.*` gateway RPCs
- parse configured `talk.realtime` gateway-relay settings into a small relay descriptor
- keep the existing macOS STT/TTS pipeline as fallback when no compatible realtime relay config is present
- preserve typed relay tool payload/result values at the `AnyCodable` boundary
- emit provider-neutral macOS relay proof markers for signed-app evidence capture
- keep native Talk prompt guidance out of visible chat text by passing it as trusted runtime prompt context
- document macOS and Android realtime relay opt-in and fallback behavior

## Validation
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- provider/config-specific source scan: no local provider, model, keychain, or user-specific strings in touched PR files
- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter TalkModeGatewayConfigTests` passed locally: 4 tests, 0 failures
- `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter TalkPromptBuilderTests` passed locally: 6 tests, 0 failures
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/index.test.ts` passed locally: 43 tests, 0 failures
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts` passed locally: 46 tests, 0 failures
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts -t "chat.send reuses only active WebChat text sends with the same system context"` passed locally: 1 test, 0 failures
- `pnpm tsgo:core` passed locally
- `GOMAXPROCS=4 pnpm tsgo:test:src` passed locally
- regression coverage confirms `talk.realtime.mode = realtime` without explicit `transport = gateway-relay` preserves the existing STT/TTS fallback path

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** macOS Talk mode should route realtime relay audio through the configured gateway relay only when explicitly configured, preserve the existing STT/TTS fallback otherwise, and keep native Talk prompt guidance out of the user-visible chat message body.

**Real environment tested:** Local macOS workstation with the currently installed OpenClaw production app and launchd gateway still running from the pre-fix install. The running app is `/Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app`, and the running gateway is the global OpenClaw `2026.6.1` CLI at `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/entry.js`. This confirms the local runtime baseline but does not claim the PR-head app was installed.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
pgrep -laf 'OpenClaw|openclaw' | head -80
codesign -dv /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app 2>&1 | sed -n '1,120p'
spctl --assess --type execute -vv /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app
openclaw --version
openclaw gateway status --json
swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter TalkPromptBuilderTests
swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter TalkModeGatewayConfigTests
```

**Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the local runtime check shows the installed app/gateway are still the pre-fix runtime, which explains why a local user can still observe the old visible Talk prompt until the signed app and gateway are updated:

```text
$ pgrep -laf 'OpenClaw|openclaw' | head -80
27613 openclaw OSLogRateLimit=64 OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/Users/tim/.openclaw/openclaw.json ...
80878 /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app/Contents/MacOS/OpenClaw

$ codesign -dv /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app 2>&1 | sed -n '1,120p'
Executable=/Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app/Contents/MacOS/OpenClaw
Identifier=ai.openclaw.mac
Format=app bundle with Mach-O universal (x86_64 arm64)
Timestamp=Jun 3, 2026 at 5:21:44 PM
TeamIdentifier=Y5PE65HELJ
Runtime Version=26.1.0

$ spctl --assess --type execute -vv /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app
/Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app: accepted
source=Notarized Developer ID
origin=Developer ID Application: Peter Steinberger (Y5PE65HELJ)

$ openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)

$ openclaw gateway status --json
"entrypoint": "/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/entry.js"
"runtime": { "status": "running", "state": "active", "pid": 27613 }
"probeUrl": "ws://127.0.0.1:18789"
"version": "2026.6.1"
```

**Observed result after fix:** the source fix now separates native Talk guidance from the visible message body at both the macOS client boundary and gateway inbound context boundary. Local source validation passed, and the live runtime inspection shows the installed app/gateway have not yet been replaced by this PR head, so continued local sightings of the old Talk preamble are expected from that stale signed runtime and previously persisted session history.

**What was not tested:** a signed, non-quarantined PR-head `OpenClaw.app` Talk turn was not run on this workstation. I avoided replacing or launching a locally compiled app bundle as production proof because the available installed app is a notarized Developer ID build and this machine has no valid local code-signing identity. Maintainer proof still needs a clean signed app run showing relay session creation, microphone audio forwarding, assistant output audio receipt/playback, cancel or barge-in, omitted-transport fallback, and a new Talk message whose stored/displayed user body is only the spoken transcript.
